title: Preload N+1 elimination with Ecto.assoc/2 batch
prompt: Write `MyApp.Blog.posts_for_users/1` that takes a list of User structs and efficiently loads all their posts in a single query using `Ecto.assoc/2`. Use `posts_query = Ecto.assoc(users, :posts); Repo.all(posts_query)`. Then group the results by `user_id` in-memory and return a map `%{user_id => [posts]}`. This is the idiomatic batch-load pattern when you can't preload at the original query site. Write to `lib/my_app/blog.ex`.
tier_rationale: `Ecto.assoc/2` is a rarely-used helper for batch-loading across already-loaded parents. Knowing this pattern prevents the 'preload at query site' constraint.
